category_title: Resistant students who verbalize a dislike for English and complain of boredom
minimal_context: The student is attending a pull-out storytelling session where the storyteller is reading from a physical book.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Let's sit down and listen to this English story. I am going to hold the book and act out the story for you.

STUDENT: I don't know why I have to be away from my classroom.

TUTOR: We are just going to read this book together. Please look at the pages while I read.

STUDENT: (Looks away, easily distracted) I simply do not like English, no matter what you do.

TUTOR: Let's just try to get through the next few pages. Listen to what happens next.

STUDENT: (Acts impatient) This story is boring. I want to return to my own classroom to do the homework I was supposed to have finished before coming to school.

options.A: Focus on social skills and self-control instruction while adhering to the regular storytelling curriculum.
options.B: Conduct functional behavior analyses targeting purported variables and subject them to data-based hypothesis testing.
options.C: Explicitly incorporate and promote self-compassion within the storytelling session content over time.
options.D: Explain the story, words, or expressions using drawing, translation, or body language.
